mnemonics.title: Business Case Components
mnemonics.content: RACE Framework: Research current state, Analyze gaps, Calculate ROI, Execute proposal with metrics

mnemonics.title: HEART Framework记忆
mnemonics.content: H-E-A-R-T = Happiness measures satisfaction, Engagement measures activity, Adoption measures new users, Retention measures loyalty, Task Success measures effectiveness

acronyms.usage: Measures financial return from UX investments - (Net Benefit / Cost) x 100
Total revenue expected from a customer throughout their relationship - impacted by UX quality
Direct measure of user satisfaction after interactions - key UX success metric
Measures customer loyalty by likelihood to recommend - indicates UX impact on advocacy

acronyms.expansion: Happiness, Engagement, Adoption, Retention, Task Success
acronyms.usage: Google's framework for measuring UX outcomes in business terms
Measurable values that demonstrate how effectively UX achieves business objectives

visualTips.visualization: Think of a funnel: Stakeholder Analysis → Problem Statement → Solution → Financial Analysis → Expected Outcomes → Approval
Pyramid: Base = Task Success & Error Rate → Middle = Engagement & Adoption → Top = Happiness & NPS → Apex = Business ROI
Iceberg model: Visible tip = support calls, complaints; Below surface = lost customers, brand damage, rework costs - much larger impact

analogies.analogy: Just as companies invest in marketing to attract customers, UX is an investment in keeping and satisfying customers - like a well-designed store that increases sales versus a confusing layout that drives customers away.
Like credit card debt that accumulates interest, design debt compounds over time - the longer you ignore poor UX decisions, the more expensive they become to fix.
Explaining UX to executives without business metrics is like showing a car engine to someone who only cares about fuel efficiency - you must speak their language (numbers, ROI).
In digital products, UX is like the waiter service in restaurants - food may be good but poor service drives customers to competitors, making UX a key differentiator.
